rounds.prompt: How many times longer is the Nile than the English Channel is wide?
rounds.fact: The English Channel is ~240 km at its widest; the Nile is ~6,650 km long — nearly 28× longer than the Channel is wide.

rounds.prompt: How many times farther is New York from London than London is from Paris?
rounds.fact: London to Paris is ~341 km; London to New York is ~5,570 km — over 16× further, crossing an entire ocean.

rounds.prompt: How many times longer is the coastline of Norway than the equator?
rounds.fact: The equator is ~40,075 km; Norway's coastline (including fjords and islands) is ~100,915 km — 2.5× the length of the equator, packed into a relatively small country.

rounds.prompt: How many times wider is Africa (at its widest) than the Amazon River is long?
rounds.fact: Africa is ~7,400 km wide at its widest; the Amazon is ~6,992 km long — almost identical. The Amazon would stretch almost all the way across Africa.

rounds.prompt: How many times could you walk around the Earth in the distance a migrating Arctic tern flies in a lifetime?

itemB.name: Arctic tern lifetime migration (~2.4 million km)

rounds.fact: Earth's circumference is ~40,075 km; an Arctic tern flies ~70,000 km/year and lives ~30 years — ~2.1 million km total, or ~53 times around Earth.
